label: OpenWeather
description: Fetch real-time weather forecasts and historical climate data from OpenWeatherMap.

form.title: Connect OpenWeather
form.subtitle: Paste your OpenWeatherMap API key to access forecasts and historical weather data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste your OpenWeatherMap API key. Free and paid keys both work — just copy it from your account dashboard and paste it here.
methods.how_to:
## Get your OpenWeatherMap API key

1. Go to [openweathermap.org](https://openweathermap.org) and sign in (or create a free account).
2. Click your username in the top-right corner and select **My API keys**.
3. A default key called `Default` is already created for new accounts. Copy it, or click **Generate** to create a named key (e.g. `Anton`).
4. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

## Notes

- New keys can take up to 2 hours to activate after account creation. If calls fail immediately, wait and retry.
- Free-tier keys give access to Current Weather, 5-day Forecast, and limited historical data. One Call API 3.0 and full historical data require a paid plan — subscribe at **Services → One Call API** in the dashboard.
- Keep your key private; anyone with it can consume your quota. Regenerate it from **My API keys** if exposed.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: 32-character key from openweathermap.org → your username → My API keys.

fields.name: api_version
fields.label: API Version
fields.description: Which OpenWeatherMap API version to use. 3.0 (One Call) requires a separate subscription.

fields.name: units
fields.label: Unit System
fields.description: Default unit system for temperature and wind speed responses.
